openparser/csv

        parseCsv

      • toString(f: CsvFieldSlice): string

This module implements a high-performance CSV parser that operates directly on memory-mapped files, providing zero-copy field slices and a callback-based API for processing rows. It supports configurable delimiters, quote characters, and strict parsing modes.

The parser is designed for large CSV files (datasets, logs, etc) that may not fit into memory, making it a great choice for data processing tasks where performance is critical.

If speed is not necessary, you can tweak the parser batchSize and batchDelayMs options to maintain a lower CPU usage in long-running parses.

Types

CsvFieldSlice = object
  data*: pointer             ## Pointer to the start of the field data in the memory-mapped file. Valid only during callback execution.
  size*: int                 ## Length of the field data in bytes.
  quoted*: bool              ## Whether the field was enclosed in quotes.
  escapedQuotes*: bool       ## Whether the field contains escaped quotes (e.g. "" within a quoted field).
  quoteChar*: char           ## The quote character used for this field (from options).
Zero-copy CSV field view into mapped memory. 
CsvOptions = object
  delimiter*: char = ','     ## The character used to separate fields (default is comma).
  quote*: char = '\"'        ## The character used to quote fields that contain special characters (e.g. delimiter, newline).
  strict*: bool = true ## If `true`, the parser will raise errors for malformed CSV (e.g. unclosed quotes, unexpected characters after closing quotes).
                       ## If `false`, the parser will attempt to recover from common CSV issues (e.g. ignore unexpected characters after closing quotes).
  batchDelayMs*: int ## Optional delay in milliseconds between batches when `batchSize` is set, allowing
                     ## for cooperative multitasking in long-running parses.
  batchSize*: int = 10000    ## Optional batching parameters for processing large files in chunks.
Configuration options for CSV parsing 
CsvParseError = object of CatchableError
CsvRowCallback = proc (fields: openArray[CsvFieldSlice]; row: int): bool {.
    closure.}
Callback type for processing parsed CSV rows. fields is an open array of CsvFieldSlice representing the fields in the row, and row is the 1-based row number. The callback should return true to continue parsing or false to stop.

Procs

proc `$`(f: CsvFieldSlice): string {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc asChars(mfile: MemFile): ptr UncheckedArray[char] {.inline, ...raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Fast raw char view over mfile.mem for pointer/index based parsers. 
proc defaultCsvOptions(): CsvOptions {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc parseCsv(mfile: MemFile; onRow: CsvRowCallback;
              options: CsvOptions = defaultCsvOptions()) {.
    ...raises: [CsvParseError, Exception], tags: [RootEffect, TimeEffect],
    forbids: [].}
High-performance CSV parser over memory-mapped files. fields are valid only during callback execution (seq is reused). 
proc parseFile(filename: string; onRow: CsvRowCallback;
               options: CsvOptions = defaultCsvOptions()) {.
    ...raises: [IOError, OSError, CsvParseError, Exception],
    tags: [ReadDirEffect, ReadIOEffect, RootEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Convenience wrapper to parse CSV files directly from disk. 
proc toString(f: CsvFieldSlice): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
